name: Bhagari Jhinga- Prawns in Tomato Cream Sauce - India
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon chili powder, or to taste
1 fresh green chili pepper, finely chopped
1 7 oz can coconut milk, well stirred, or cream
1 1/4 lbs medium-sized uncooked prawns, peeled and deveined
recipeInstructions:
To make the sauce, put tomato puree in a bowl. Add salt, sugar, Garam Masala, ground roasted cumin seeds, chili powder, coriander leaves, green chili, lemon juice, and about 2 tablespoons water; mix well. Slowly add coconut milk or cream, mixing until smooth. Set aside.
To stir-fry the prawns, heat oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add mustard seeds and wait for them to sputter. Once the seeds begin to pop, add garlic and curry leaves. Stir-fry until the garlic turns medium brown, then add the prawns. Cook until the prawns turn opaque throughout. Pour in the sauce mixture and lower the heat to medium. Allow it to simmer gently until the sauce is heated through, which should take about 5-7 minutes. The prawns should be cooked through by this time. Turn off the heat and serve with rice.
